# Contributing to Boarddy

First off, thank you for checking out Boarddy and taking the time to contribute!

## Proprietary Software Notice

Boarddy is commercial software owned by **Huna Inc.** and is **not open source**. As a result:
* The core codebase, compiled binaries, and UI designs are proprietary.
* We **do not accept external code Pull Requests** that modify the core application logic.
* Public access to the code is for build transparency, packaging validation, and community issues/discussions.

---

## How You Can Contribute

Even though Boarddy is proprietary, we value our community's input! Here are the ways you can contribute:

### 1. Reporting Bugs
If you find a bug, please check the [existing issues](https://github.com/boarddy-io/boarddy/issues) first. If it hasn't been reported, open a new issue using our **Bug Report** template. Be sure to include:
* Your Operating System (Windows, macOS, Linux) and version.
* Boarddy version.
* Clear steps to reproduce the issue.
* Expected vs. actual behavior.

### 2. Requesting Features & Ideas
We want Boarddy to be the ultimate input and memory layer for your workflow. If you have ideas or feature requests:
* Open a discussion in the **Ideas** or **Feature Requests** category of [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/boarddy-io/boarddy/discussions).
* Share details on the problem you're trying to solve and how the feature would work.
* Upvote and comment on ideas shared by other users.

### 3. Feedback on Developer Mode
If you use Boarddy's developer tools (e.g., snippet integration, shortcuts, command expansion):
* Share suggestions in the **Developer Mode** category of GitHub Discussions.
* Suggest additions for vim-like bindings, developer shortcuts, or custom commands.

### 4. Improving Documentation
Spot a typo in the README? Want to write a guide on integrating Boarddy with your text editor?
* Open a issue using the **Documentation Improvement** template.
* For documentation files (like guides, FAQs, and wiki items), we *do* welcome documentation-only Pull Requests!

### 5. Translation & Multilingual Support
Boarddy supports typing in multiple languages. If you'd like to help translate the interface or improve dictionaries for your native language:
* Reach out to us via [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/boarddy-io/boarddy/discussions) or email us at [support@huna.io](mailto:support@huna.io).

---

## Community Standards

All contributors and community members are expected to follow our [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) in all discussions and issue comments.

Thank you for being part of the Boarddy journey!
